messages.content:
Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“When thinking abit this question, the first news that comes to my mind is my algorithm engineering partner has got his pollution last month to be more specific, as long as he got the news he told me at first and to my surprise, it's uh it's a quite high level for him and I send my congratulations to him first and then we decided to celebrate after our job and talking about his job job he he really well in algorithm and he lead us in a program which achieve our business goal in the and brought a lot of profit t our business and companies company. and and in the futures I would like to cooperate with him for for a long time because not only he's expertise in algorithm and benefit our business but also close buddies in our daily life and we go to gym together and help each other.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Describe a piece of good news that you heard about someone you know well
You should say:

What it was

When you heard it

How you knew it

And explain how you felt about it”

When I reflect on this question, the piece of good news that immediately springs to mind involves my algorithm engineering partner. Last month, he shared with me that he had achieved a significant promotion, a detail he revealed almost as soon as he learned of it himself. His accomplishment took me by surprise due to the high level he had reached, and I was among the first to congratulate him. We later decided to celebrate this milestone after work.

I heard about his promotion directly from him, highlighting the close communication and camaraderie we share. His excellence in algorithm development not only led our project to success, fulfilling our business objectives and significantly boosting profits for our company, but it also underscored his pivotal role within our team.

My feelings upon hearing this news were a mix of pride and happiness for his achievement. His expertise in algorithms has not only been a significant advantage for our business but has also paved the way for a strong personal bond between us. We've become close friends, frequently visiting the gym together and supporting each other in various aspects of our lives.

Looking towards the future, I am eager to continue our collaboration for a long time. His proficiency and the positive impact on our work, combined with our friendship, make this professional relationship both valuable and rewarding.
Thank you. Come up with the spoken version of this.

Sure, here's how you might say this out loud in a more conversational tone for a spoken presentation:

"So, when I was asked about a piece of good news I recently heard concerning someone close to me, my mind instantly went to my partner in algorithm engineering. Just last month, he shared some fantastic news with me—he'd been promoted, which honestly caught me off guard because of how significant the promotion was. Of course, I was thrilled for him and didn't waste a moment to congratulate him. We even decided to celebrate the big win once we finished up work for the day.

I found out about his promotion straight from him, which really shows the tight-knit relationship we've got. I mean, his skills in algorithm development have not only played a crucial role in our project's success, meeting our business goals and boosting our company's profits, but they've also made him an indispensable part of our team.

Feeling about it? I was over the moon for him. It's not just about him being a genius in what he does and bringing so much to our business; it's also about the strong friendship we've built. We're gym buddies, we support each other—it goes beyond work.

And looking ahead, I'm totally looking forward to working with him for the long haul. It's this blend of professional brilliance and personal camaraderie that really makes me value our partnership. It's just awesome, and I feel pretty lucky to have that kind of connection in my professional life."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“Well, that's an interesting question. From my perspective, the kids has have a lot of occasion that have to make their own decisions which is quire vital for their whole life. Firstly, when they choose the school in the early life it has to be a big decision but but in that case at that time, their parents help them in the very early age. Secondly, children have to when children grow up they want to know what major and job they want to do in the future. As a result, they need to think about their interests and strengths. In a nutshell, children have a lot of big decisions in their life.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Do you think children ever have to make important decisions?”

"Certainly, the question of whether children must make important decisions is thought-provoking. In my view, children encounter numerous pivotal decisions throughout their lives. For instance, choosing a school represents one of the initial significant choices in their early years, even though parents primarily guide this decision due to the children's young age. As children mature, they face the critical task of identifying their preferred major and future career path. This requires them to consider their interests and strengths thoughtfully. In summary, children indeed confront several crucial decisions that can shape their entire lives."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“This is a tough question. From my perspective, teenagers after their graduation are change their roles to the adults. So, main decisions they have to make is to have to choose their career path and besides, when they get to the job, they may feel different various individuals and someone may feel their are suitable for their job and others feel twice. Apart from that, young adults have to make some have to make make some decision about he or she should marry with someone or they can be single for whole life and across the board, I think job and wedding are two big decisions adults have to make.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What important decisions do teenagers need to make after graduation?”

"This is indeed a challenging question. In my view, upon graduation, teenagers stand at the cusp of adulthood and are faced with pivotal decisions. Primarily, they must choose their career path, a choice that shapes their professional life. Entering the workforce introduces them to diverse people and situations, making some feel right at home in their chosen job, while others may have second thoughts. Beyond career choices, young adults also face personal decisions, such as whether to marry or remain single. All in all, career and marital choices represent two of the most significant decisions that adults must make."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“Well, I think it depends on children's life stage and the firstly, hmm some for some babies, they can can make decision by their parents because they are on very early stage and secondly some teenagers they always live and study at school they can ask their teacher for help. but for some young adults, no matter if they study in university or work in society, they may require their friends or bosses for help and to make the long story short, I think it's different parts of different parts of youngsters ways and role for ask for help.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Who can children turn to for help when making a decision?”

"Well, I believe the availability of help for children in decision-making varies depending on their stage in life. For very young children, such as infants, parents naturally make decisions on their behalf due to their infancy. As children grow into teenagers and spend more time at school, they often seek guidance from their teachers. Meanwhile, young adults, whether they are pursuing higher education or starting their careers, might turn to friends or supervisors for advice. To summarize, the sources of support shift according to the different phases of youth and their evolving roles in seeking assistance."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
From my perspective, it really deoends on what stage of my life and my early life my primary school and middle school I would like to study with my classmate and I used to study in the class with the teacher with teaching knowledge everyday. besides, when I grow up, I don't have enough time when I study every day and I have to study by myself and in that case, I prefer to study alone which can improve my study efficiency and effectiveness and across the board, I think the ways to learn is according to the situation and the life stage people have to face.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Do you like to learn on your own or with others?”

"From my perspective, my preference for learning either on my own or with others greatly depends on my life stage. During my early years in primary and middle school, I enjoyed studying with my classmates. Our learning was facilitated by teachers who shared their knowledge with us daily in the classroom. However, as I grew older and my schedule became more demanding, I found that I had less time for group study sessions. Consequently, I shifted towards studying independently, a method I discovered could significantly enhance both my study efficiency and effectiveness. In essence, I believe that the most effective learning method varies according to one's current situation and stage in life."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
That's really a tough question I never thought about it before but from what I have witnessed people who have a lot of work experience are more targetable about their study schedule and for example, if a if a when a student leave their school and get into a job they all face a lot of difficulties everyday on their work and they know about their client demands and business objective. In that case, they can quickly they can easily know their weakness of their knowledge so they have a clear direction of what to learn and they can learn swiftly in their classes but for the student who have working experience, they have big weakness is the time they don't have enough opportunity to stay in the classroom everyday so the time is the most pressure for them. in a nutshell, working experience is a double-edged sword for people who want to study.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What are the benfits of gaining work experience while studying?”

"That's indeed a challenging question, and until now, I hadn't given it much thought. However, based on what I've observed, individuals with substantial work experience tend to be more strategic about their study schedules. For instance, when a student transitions from school to the workforce, they encounter numerous challenges in their job daily. They become acutely aware of client demands and business objectives, which positions them to identify gaps in their knowledge more readily. As a result, they have a clear direction for their learning, enabling them to absorb information more efficiently during their studies. However, students with work experience face a significant hurdle: time. Balancing job responsibilities often leaves them with limited opportunities to engage in classroom learning, making time a precious commodity. In summary, while work experience can sharpen focus and accelerate learning, it also presents the challenge of time management for those wishing to further their education."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“My boss asked me to spearhead a team to investigate a target company only two weeks before the deadline which my manager demanded. Besides, I had to visit the plant site to collect valuable information to support my final report. However, the time leaving to me was only 1 week.”

"My boss tasked me with leading a team to conduct a due diligence investigation on a target company, with a strict deadline set by my manager for just two weeks. Additionally, I was required to visit the plant site to gather essential information for my final report. Unfortunately, by the time I was able to start, I had only one week left to complete everything."
Thank you. Come up with the spoken version of this.

"Sure, so here's the deal: My boss comes up to me and says, 'I need you to head up a team, and you guys are going to dive deep into checking out this company we're interested in. Oh, and you've got two weeks because that's what the manager wants.' On top of that, he tells me I've got to actually go over to the company's plant site myself to pick up some crucial info we need for our final report. But catch this – by the time I'm actually able to get started on all of this, I've only got one week left to pull everything together."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“To catch up the deadline and made the visit site more efficiency, I needed to prepare fully before the visit so therefore, I booked the meeting I booked the call meeting with the target company in advance to make the detailed schedule. Meanwhile, I asked the CEO's time to collect to get the exact requirement from management side. Finally, I only spend two days finishing the whole visit in the plant site and moreover, forming the final report submitted to CEO before the deadline.”

"To meet the deadline and make the site visit more efficient, I fully prepared in advance. I arranged a meeting with the target company well ahead of time to plan a detailed schedule. Concurrently, I secured a time to discuss with the CEO and gather the specific requirements from the management side. As a result, I was able to complete the entire site visit in just two days and, furthermore, succeeded in submitting the final report to the CEO before the deadline."
Thank you. Make it a bit shorter and say it in the spoken version.

"To meet the deadline and streamline the site visit, I prepped ahead, scheduling a call with the target company to plan everything out. I also checked in with our CEO to understand exactly what management was looking for. Amazingly, I wrapped up the site visit in just two days and got the final report to our CEO before the deadline."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“Although it was a rush time, sometimes I feel overwhelmed and exhausted. However, the final result made me feel a kind of accomplishment and satisfied. Besides, the leaders appreciated a lot since I stopped potential loss in my company.”

"Despite the rush and feeling overwhelmed and exhausted at times, the final outcome left me with a sense of accomplishment and satisfaction. Moreover, the leaders expressed significant appreciation because I was able to prevent potential losses for my company."
